title: Duvalia concolor
taxonomy: Duvalia concolor is classified within the family Apocynaceae. This family comprises a large group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Duvalia concolor is further classified within the genus Duvalia, which includes other species of plants native to the deserts of southern Africa. The exact classification of Duvalia concolor is as follows: Apocynaceae > Asclepiadoideae > Duvalia > Duvalia concolor.

intro: Duvalia concolor, also known as the Star of the Desert or Moonstone Cactus, is a species of flowering plant in the genus Duvalia, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis, diabetes, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Duvalia concolor include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, including duvalin and duvaline, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are infusions and decoctions, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, as well as in skincare products to harness its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
